quotes.verbatim_quote:
Options:
© Revoice the chords of the modal progression as hybrids.
@ Use the root motion from the modal progression as the root motion of a
constant structure pattern.
e Use pedal tones or ostinato patterns to support any progression produced by
these means.

Choose a song fragment that contains a diatonic melody. The fragment below
could be diatonic fo more than one key.

The use of an A pedal in the example below makes the phrase sound as if it is
in the key of A, despite the ambiguous nature of thé constant structure progression.

If you seek a strong modal quality, repeat the | chord
and its characteristic cadential chords offen to drive the point home. Use of a tonic pedal
tone or tonic ostinato will help to reinforce the sense of-modal tonality.
quotes.why_load_bearing: How to make modal progression convincing.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Modal phrases and constant structure examples normally require repetition io develop a
clear meaning in the listener’s ear.

Hybrid chords may occasionally be used as isolated structures within phrases, but they
acquire a stronger textural personality when used in a series. A series of hybrid chords
produces a stronger impressionistic effect than an isolated hybrid voicing.

Use the root motion from the modal progression as the root motion of a
constant structure pattern.

Two other considerations were important in this choice:
1. All melody notes within this fragment are diatonic to A minor.

2. The melody ends on A in measure 3, which reinforces the modal character of

the phrase.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Selection criteria for modal root motion.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Voicing all or some of the chords in the modal progression as hybrid structures develops
the chord progression further.

This introduces some variety, yet maintains a similar chordal texture
throughout the phrase.
